judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content (infrared photo, crosshair reticle, horizontal scale with numbers, dark dot to the right, terrain at bottom, redactions, timestamp), but Description B provides a specific timestamp text ('12/31/99 18:10:50') that Description A omits.
original_image_tags: A black-and-white infrared (IR) sensor still, likely from a military or surveillance system. The image displays a central crosshair reticle with vertical and horizontal scales marked with tick marks and numbers. The horizontal axis is labeled with numbers: "15", "10", "5" on the left side of center and "5", "10", "15" on the right. A small, dark, distinct dot is visible just to the right of the center crosshair, positioned near the "1" mark on the horizontal axis and slightly above the horizontal centerline. The background is a uniform, grainy gray tone, typical of IR imagery, with a darker, uneven landmass or horizon visible at the very bottom edge. Multiple rectangular redaction blocks are placed across the image, obscuring specific areas: a large black bar across the top edge; several black and gray blocks on the left and right sides, aligned roughly with the horizontal scale; and additional redaction blocks near the bottom corners. A timestamp is visible in the bottom left corner.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: Grainy infrared-style photograph with a centered white crosshair and horizontal/vertical tick marks labeled 5, 10, and 15; a small dark dot appears just right of the vertical axis above center, with several black redaction bars overlaid around the edges and a timestamp reading “12/31/99 18:10:50” at bottom left.*
